## reference · app store promo screenshot design best practices narrative conversion

- **Base upload requirement (late 2024 onward):** submit **one 6.9‑inch iPhone set** and **one 13‑inch iPad set**; Apple auto‑scales to all older iPhone/iPad models.  
- **Recommended base dimensions:**  
  - 6.9‑inch iPhone portrait = **1290 × 2796 px** (landscape = 2796 × 1290 px).  
  - 6.5‑inch iPhone portrait = **1284 × 2778 px** (landscape = 2778 × 1284 px) – optional backup.  
  - 13‑inch iPad portrait = **2064 × 2752 px**.  
- **Screenshot count:** up to **10 screenshots** per device type **per localization**; **minimum 1** per supported device family (iPhone + iPad).  
- **File format & color:** **PNG** (preferred) or **JPEG**; **RGB only**; no alpha/transparency. PNG preserves UI text sharpness.  
- **Resolution & quality:** capture at native resolution; **do not upscale**, add black bars, or pad. Review at **100 % zoom** before upload.  
- **Orientation rule:** match the app’s UI orientation; **portrait for portrait apps, landscape for landscape apps**; do **not mix orientations** within the same device family.  
- **Safe‑area compliance:** keep UI elements away from edges to avoid clipping on edge‑to‑edge displays.  
- **Content authenticity:** screenshots must show **real UI**; no exaggerated, salesy, or fabricated claims.  
- **Prohibited text/elements:**  
  - No CTAs such as “Download Now”, “Install today”, “Limited offer”.  
  - No hype tags like “#1 App”, “Best App”, “Top rated”.  
  - No external URLs, QR codes, email addresses, social‑media handles, or cross‑platform references (e.g., Google Play).  
  - No real user names, phone numbers, or private data – use placeholders.  
- **Allowed textual claims:** only **verifiable, recent awards** or genuine statements; keep copy clear, neutral, and free of idioms/slang.  
- **Original assets only:** do not use copyrighted images, icons, or data; create **original content** for all screenshots.  
- **Localization:** screenshots are language‑specific metadata; each storefront needs its own set (up to 10).  
  - Adapt **currencies, date formats, measurement units** to the target market.  
  - Use culturally appropriate colors, symbols, and imagery.  
  - Allocate extra space for longer texts (e.g., German, French).  
  - Prioritize localized content in the **first 2–3 screenshots** for maximum conversion lift.  
- **Narrative flow recommendation:** structure screenshots to convey **Problem → Solution → Outcome** rather than dense text blocks.  
- **Technical checklist before submission:**  
  1. Verify dimensions match the base sizes above.  
  2. Confirm file type (PNG/JPEG) and RGB color space.  
  3. Ensure no transparency or black bars.  
  4. Review at 100 % zoom for clipping or scaling artifacts.  
  5. Check that all text/claims comply with the “no hype/CTA” rules.  
  6. Validate localization per storefront (up to 10 screenshots each).  

Sources: https://theapplaunchpad.com/blog/app-store-screenshot-guidelines/
